extraction.one_line_summary: BALLERINI Davide won final sprint in Krakow after ACKERMANN Pascal opened too early, while EVENEPOEL Remco secured overall by 1:52.
tactical_patterns: STUYVEN Jasper opening sprint as Classics specialist without pure sprinter speed allows late-comers like BALLERINI to capitalize.
Trek-Segafredo worked hardest to chase breakaway for ACKERMANN but used STUYVEN leadout, revealing no dedicated sprint train depth.
CAVENDISH Mark mechanicals with 1km remaining suggests positioning too far back before finale on technical circuit laps.

rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: positive: first WorldTour win shows timing ability to pass early launchers, wait until ACKERMANN faded after long sprint
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: despite in-form status and Trek chasing for him, launching behind STUYVEN burned him before line
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 1:52 GC margin after Stage 4 attack shows first WorldTour stage race win with mature time management
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: opening sprint as Classics rider for team without pure sprinter reveals Trek's sprint deficit despite chase work
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: unclipping pedal 1km out on technical circuit reveals positioning or bike-handling vulnerability after layoff
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: third place finish without visible leadout shows raw speed competitive with established sprinters
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: second overall at 1:52 confirms inability to follow EVENEPOEL's 50km Stage 4 attack despite experience
